Title: Bones (alpha test)
Description:
Current version is v0.0.4

v0.0.4 has some hefty serialization changes. A new save is recommended, so downdate to finish off old runs. It also adds an option to unsilence a lot of the silenced log errors during deserialization. It's not recommended, especially if you have "Errors as Popups" enabled.

v0.0.3.9 isn't compatible with bones files that are from version prior to it and won't attempt to load them even with the option set to allow it.

If you're on a version lower than v0.0.3.6, it's recommended you update to v0.0.3.6 since earlier versions of the mod can't load bones from it but will still try to. The OsseousAsh cloud has been "wiped" (although the previous database has been preserved).

[h3]If you're using Linux, switch to the 'linuxtest' beta to enable network capabilities[/h3]

Adds a Bones folder to the game's sync directory which gets filled with the bones of runs that end too soon, so that they might have one last shot at the moon.

Alpha test for what amounts to a prototype. Requires at least beta version 2.0.211.36 (won't work on main, won't work on lang).

[h2]Online Bones[/h2]
[h3]Now supports online bones. Make sure the default host is disabled outside of closed beta's, or you risk the server banning your IP![/h3]
Contact me here or on discord (@.underdoug) if you suspect this has occured; I'll sort you out. 

Privately/independantly hosted servers are exempt from this warning, but are outside of my control. Server source is available on my github, linked below.

Online bones make http(s) requests to a RESTful API, hosted on a remote (or local) server, designed to serve bones info and bones save files directly into your game, and to upload them when you die (entirely Opt-In).

[h3]Moderation[/h3]
Includes a report system for bones that are buggy, griefing, contain offensive content, or are problematic for reasons up to you to describe.

This was the easiest (at first) moderation solution, since closed betas are small and writing proper, automatic moderation is not trivial.

It won't be the ultimate solution, but should suffice for the immediate time being.

[h2]Source[/h2]
Go to the [url=https://github.com/UnderDoug/UD_Bones_Folder]git repository[/url] for a zip file (currently out of date, contact me if you're interested in closed beta access for the cloud bones) with some test bones, or to get a look at the guts.

Don't be shy about posting log dumps into the discussion thread labelled for them.

If you like what I'm doing or something I've made, or have some ideas you think could improve the mod, you should let me know!
